article_title: Nutshell CRM Review 2026: Is It the Best Simple CRM for Small Teams?
twitter_thread: Small teams drowning in complex CRM software? You're not alone. 73% of small businesses say their CRM is too complicated for what they actually need. Here's what we found testing Nutshell CRM for 30 days 👇

Nutshell CRM nails the basics: Clean sales pipeline, contact management, and email tracking without the bloat. Perfect for teams under 20 who need results, not another project. The catch? Limited automation for scaling beyond basics.

Is Nutshell right for your team? We tested pricing, features, and real-world usability. Full breakdown here: https://saaspulsemedia.com/articles/nutshell-crm-review-2026-is-it-the-best-simple-crm-for-small-0ulu.html
twitter_single: Nutshell CRM: The straightforward, affordable sales pipeline tool small teams actually enjoy using. But is simple too simple? Our 2026 review: https://saaspulsemedia.com/articles/nutshell-crm-review-2026-is-it-the-best-simple-crm-for-small-0ulu.html
linkedin_post:
Most CRMs are built for enterprise teams with dedicated admins. But what if you're a small team that just needs to track deals and close sales?

We spent 30 days testing Nutshell CRM to see if "simple" can still be powerful.

The verdict: Nutshell excels at what small teams actually need—intuitive sales pipeline management, solid contact organization, and email tracking that doesn't require a PhD to configure. The pricing is refreshingly transparent, and onboarding takes hours, not weeks.

The tradeoff? If you're planning aggressive growth or need sophisticated automation workflows, you might outgrow it faster than expected. But for teams under 20 focused on core sales activities without enterprise complexity, it's a compelling option.

We tested features, pricing tiers, integrations, and compared it against alternatives. Bottom line: sometimes simple wins.

Full review and feature breakdown: https://saaspulsemedia.com/articles/nutshell-crm-review-2026-is-it-the-best-simple-crm-for-small-0ulu.html
